metadata.title: Vectra Set Detection Status
metadata.description: This playbook updates the status of all detections listed in an incident's custom details based on the current incident status. When the incident is Active, detections are set to 'acknowledged'. When the incident is Closed, detections are closed with a reason derived from the incident close classification (True Positive → remediated; Benign Positive / False Positive / Undetermined → benign).
prerequisites: 1. The Vectra XDR data connector should be configured to create alerts and generate an incident based on entity data in Microsoft Sentinel.
2. Obtain KeyVault name and TenantId where client credentials are stored:
a. Create a Key Vault with a unique name.
b. Go to Keyvaults → <your keyvault> → Overview and copy DirectoryID which will be used as TenantId.
NOTE: Make sure the Permission model in the Access Configuration of Keyvault is set to 'Vault access policy'.
3. Deploy VectraGenerateAccessToken playbook before deploying this playbook.
4. The analytical rule must include Custom Details with at minimum 'detection_id' field mapped from Vectra alert data.
postDeployment: **a. Authorize connections**
Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection.
1. Go to your Logic App → API connections → Select the Keyvault connection resource.
2. Go to General → Edit API connection.
3. Click Authorize → Sign in → Save.
**b. Add Access policy in Key Vault**
1. Go to Logic App → Identity → System assigned and copy Object (principal) ID.
2. Go to Key Vault → Access policies → Create.
3. Select all Keys & Secrets permissions → Next.
4. Search by the copied Object ID in the Principal section → Next → Review + create.
5. Repeat steps 2–4 for the user account used to authorize the connection.
**c. Assign Role to update incident**
1. Go to Log Analytics Workspace → <your workspace> → Access Control → Add role assignment.
2. Assignment type: Job function roles.
3. Role: Microsoft Sentinel Contributor.
4. Members: select managed identity and add your Logic App as member → Review + assign.
**d. Configurations in Microsoft Sentinel**
1. The analytical rule must be configured to trigger an incident based on Vectra data. The incident must include Entity mapping and Custom Details with 'detection_id' field.
2. To manually run the playbook on a particular incident:
a. Go to Microsoft Sentinel → Incidents.
b. Select an incident.
c. In the right pane, click Actions → Run Playbook.
d. Click Run beside this playbook.

metadata.description: Please do not keep 'PlaybookName' parameter empty, else you will receive validation failure
Name of the Key Vault storing the Vectra access token secret
Azure AD Directory (Tenant) ID used for Key Vault authentication
Name of the deployed VectraGenerateAccessToken playbook (e.g. VectraGenerateAccessToken)

runError.message: No Alerts Found associated with this Incident.
Playbook Triggered from Wrong Incident. Custom Details with 'detection_id' field is required.
No Detection IDs found in incident Custom Details. Ensure the 'detection_id' field is mapped in the analytical rule.
Error occurred while generating access token for updating detection status.
Error occurred while updating detection status in Vectra.

body.message:
Vectra Set Detection Status — Completed
Incident Status: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['status']}
@{if(equals(triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['status'], 'Closed'), concat('Incident Classification: ', triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['classification'], '
Detection Close Reason: ', variables('detection_close_reason')), 'Detection Status set to: acknowledged')}
Detection IDs updated: @{variables('detection_ids')}
